mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains unchanged, real yields fall and equity valuations contract, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a monthly increase in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate earnings, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) outside recovery regimes or reverses direction in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income after taxes increases household purchasing power and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Spearman rho < 0.05) with RSAFS growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year drop in housing starts signals weakening construction demand and reduced economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting equity valuations through higher earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-proxy models (Sharpe ratio difference < 0.1) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to raise policy rates, increasing discount rates and causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of high-inflation observations within tightening regimes over a 4-year test.
